title: The Four People in Your Feed (and How to Not Lose Your Mind)
hook: Patanjali predicted your Instagram feed 2,000 years ago. He also told you exactly how to survive it.

script.visual: Fast thumb-scroll through a phone: engagement photo, someone crying in a car, a gym bro flexing, a comment war. Hard cut to face.
script.voiceover: Four types of people are running through your feed right now. And your mind is getting wrecked by all of them.

script.visual: Split into four quadrants appearing one-by-one: happy couple engagement, person venting, marathon finisher, online troll.
script.voiceover: Patanjali noticed this 2,000 years before TikTok. Sutra 1.33. He says the mind meets four kinds of people: the happy, the suffering, the virtuous, and the wicked. And for each one, your default reaction is slightly wrong.

script.visual: Close on the first quadrant: the engagement photo. Then the crying one. Quick, surgical cuts.
script.voiceover: You see someone happy — your engagement-ring friend — and you get jealous. Patanjali says: Maitri. Friendliness. Feel the joy with them instead of measuring yourself against them. You see someone suffering — the 2 AM venting text — and you feel pity, or you scroll past. He says: Karuna. Compassion. Not pity. Presence.
script.text_overlay: MAITRI (friendliness) toward the happy / KARUNA (compassion) toward the suffering

script.visual: Third quadrant: the marathon finisher. Fourth: a toxic comment thread. Person reacting.
script.voiceover: You see someone doing the right thing — the friend who actually got up at 5 AM — and you feel small. He says: Mudita. Delight. Be happy they are doing well. And the wicked one — the troll, the ex, the boss who lied? Upeksha. Equanimity. Not forgiveness. Not hatred. Just... not letting them live rent-free in your head.
script.text_overlay: MUDITA (delight) toward the virtuous / UPEKSHA (equanimity) toward the wicked

script.visual: Phone placed face-down on a table. Person exhales.
script.voiceover: Four attitudes. One result. Chitta prasadanam — the mind becomes clear. Your feed is a mental gym. Use these four reps.
Of the four — which one is hardest for you? Be honest.
script.text_overlay: Which of the 4 is hardest? Comment.

cta: Which attitude do you fail at most — joy for the happy, compassion for the suffering, delight for the virtuous, or equanimity toward the wicked? Drop it below.
caption:
Patanjali diagnosed your Instagram feed 2,000 years before it existed.

Sutra 1.33: मैत्रीकरुणामुदितोपेक्षाणां सुखदुःखपुण्यापुण्यविषयाणां भावनातश्चित्तप्रसादनम्
maitri-karuna-mudita-upekshanam sukha-duhkha-punya-apunya-vishayanam bhavanatash chitta-prasadanam

"By cultivating friendliness toward the happy, compassion toward the suffering, delight toward the virtuous, and equanimity toward the wicked — the mind becomes clear."

Four people are in your feed every day:

1. The happy one (engagement photo) — don't compare. Feel the joy with them. MAITRI.
2. The suffering one (2 AM text) — don't scroll past. Be there. KARUNA.
3. The virtuous one (actually working on themselves) — don't shrink. Celebrate them. MUDITA.
4. The wicked one (troll, liar, ex) — don't hate. Don't engage. UPEKSHA.

One sutra. Four reps. A clean mind.

Which one are you weakest at?

#yogasutras #patanjali #yogadarshan #maitri #karuna #mudita #upeksha #socialmedia #attentioneconomy #mindfulness #consciousness #ancientwisdom #instagram #mentalhealth #philosophy
music_suggestion: Start with frantic social-media scroll sound for 3s, drop into a steady lo-fi loop that slows and clears as each attitude is introduced. End on a single sustained breath-like drone.
